title: cedrus libanitica subsp. libani
intro: Cedrus libanitica subsp. libani, also known as the Lebanese cedar, is a species of coniferous tree native to the mountain ranges of Lebanon. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its essential oil and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cedrus libanitica subsp. libani include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the essential oil of Cedrus libanitica subsp. libani is often used in aromatherapy, as a topical application for skin issues, and as a natural insect repellent.
